- Go UP from cols 29-33, rows 40-41 → cols 29-33, rows 35-36 (stuck)

- Go UP from cols 34-38, rows 40-41 → cols 34-38, rows 5-6 (8 UP actions)
- Go RIGHT from cols 34-38, rows 5-6 → cols 49-53, rows 5-6 (3 RIGHT actions)
- Go DOWN from cols 49-53, rows 5-6 → cols 49-53, rows 20-21 (3 DOWN actions)
- Go UP from cols 49-53, rows 20-21 → cols 49-53, rows 10-11 (2 UP actions) → game over
The game ended after reaching cols 49-53, rows 10-11. But the level was not completed.

Actually, the game ended after 2 UP actions from cols 49-53, rows 20-21. The blocks moved to cols 49-53, rows 10-11. But the game ended.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks at cols 29-33, rows 40-41
# Try DOWN to see if blocks can go down

result = action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After DOWN - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break
print("Game over:", result.get('game_over', False))

# Check area below blocks
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        r_min = n['boundary'][0][0]
        c_min = n['boundary'][0][1]
        c_max = n['boundary'][1][1]
        print(f"\nBelow orange (rows {r_min+2}-{r_min+8}, cols {c_min-2}-{c_max+6}):")
        for r in range(r_min+2, r_min+9):
            if 0 <= r < len(lines):
                print(f"  {r}: {lines[r][c_min-2:c_max+6]}")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
